evals.prompt: I'm starting a new TypeScript SaaS project for dental practice management. It'll use Next.js 16 with App Router, Clerk for auth, Supabase for DB, and Stripe for billing. I need a proper repo structure that enforces module boundaries. Can you set up the project structure?
evals.expected_output: Should produce a complete A-G output with canonical directory tree including src/modules/ for auth, billing, offices, db modules. Should include ESLint no-restricted-imports config for SDK boundary enforcement. Should recommend SaaSStandards co-activation.

assertions.name: includes-modules-directory
assertions.description: Output includes src/modules/ directory with at least auth, billing, and db modules

assertions.name: includes-eslint-config
assertions.description: Output includes ESLint no-restricted-imports configuration for SDK boundaries

assertions.name: recommends-saas-standards
assertions.description: Output mentions SaaSStandards skill activation for auth and onboarding patterns

assertions.name: includes-check-structure-script
assertions.description: Output includes check-structure.sh or equivalent CI health check

evals.prompt: Audit the repo at ~/Projects/scratch — I want to know if the structure is healthy, what framework it best matches, and what needs fixing. Give me a full health report.
evals.expected_output: Should scan the repo structure, classify against 8 framework archetypes, run health checks (structural, metadata if applicable, unknown-bucket, module boundary if src/modules exists), produce a scored report with FAIL/WARN/INFO breakdown, and offer to create a refactor plan if score is low.

assertions.name: scans-directory-structure
assertions.description: Agent actually scans the repo structure using Glob or similar tools

assertions.name: classifies-framework
assertions.description: Output classifies the repo against one or more of the 8 framework archetypes

assertions.name: produces-health-score
assertions.description: Output includes a numerical health score or pass/fail breakdown

assertions.name: provides-recommendations
assertions.description: Output includes prioritized recommendations for structural improvements

evals.prompt: My transcript-library repo at ~/Projects/transcript-library is messy. I want to restructure it. Create a refactor plan with a Move Map, but I'm running this from Codex CLI so keep that in mind for execution options.
evals.expected_output: Should detect the agent as codex-cli (or handle the stated context), scan current structure, select target framework, generate Move Map with phased migration. In Step 8 (Present and Ask), should NOT offer 'Execute via Codex' option since we're already in Codex — should offer 'Execute directly' instead.

assertions.name: acknowledges-codex-context
assertions.description: Output acknowledges or adapts to the Codex CLI execution context

assertions.name: generates-move-map
assertions.description: Output includes a Move Map table with old/new paths and categories

assertions.name: no-circular-delegation
assertions.description: Output does NOT offer 'Execute via Codex' as an option (would be circular)

assertions.name: offers-direct-execution
assertions.description: Output offers direct execution or save-plan-only as options
